# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees — Regular Meeting
### May 15, 2018

> **⚠️ Disclaimer:** These are **unofficial notes** based on an auto-generated transcript of the board meeting. They may contain errors or omissions. Please consult official minutes for the authoritative record.

---

## Opening
- Meeting called to order with a quorum present.
- Presentation of colors by NJROTC cadets (Lt. Commander Cortes, Cadet Lt. JG Vanderhorst, Cadet PO3 Landboat, Cadet CPO Rafiki Kitty, Cadet CPO Sterner).
- Pledge of Allegiance led by Mr. Hubert.
- Invocation led by Mr. Hubert.

---

## Item 2: Special District Recognition — Patrons Influencing Education (PIE) Award

**Presenter:** Dr. Hines

**Recipient:** Assistance League of Montgomery County

**Key Details:**
- Through its **Operation School Bell** program, the Assistance League provided new clothing to **1,760 Conroe ISD students** (K–12) this school year, spending over **$176,000**.
  - K–8 students receive $90; high school students receive $125 in clothing.
  - Students are invited to "dressing events" at local stores where League members assist and pay for clothing.
  - In 2016–17, the program dressed **1,935 students** and spent over **$177,000**.
- The **Legacy of Literacy** program provided **$2,000 each to 10 CISD schools** for library books, totaling nearly **$20,000** this year.
- Total spent on Conroe ISD students this year: **$196,000**; two-year total: over **$373,000**.
- Funding comes from the Assistance League Thrift Shop in Conroe and donations/grants.
- The organization operates with **100% volunteers** — no paid staff.
- **President Janet Chien** accepted the award on behalf of the organization.
- Dr. Stockton commended the organization and recognized former and current Operation School Bell/Literacy program chairs in attendance.

---

## Item 3: Public Participation

### Speaker 1: Dana Anderson
- Spoke as a concerned citizen and grandparent of an autistic child regarding **David Simms**, a 12-year-old autistic 5th grader at **Bozeman Intermediate School**.
- Alleged that David was **removed from class, arrested, handcuffed, and transported to Montgomery County Juvenile Detention Center** without his parents being contacted.
- Alleged David had been **constantly bullied** at school without repercussions; other students involved in the incident were not arrested.
- Alleged David was subjected to a **urine test** without parental knowledge.
- Stated David's mother was **refused access to full school records** and was treated rudely by the principal, who hung up on her.
- Stated that a request for David's **arrest record** from the CISD Police Department was denied.
- **Requested a full investigation** and asked that the matter be added to the **June 2018 board agenda**.

### Speaker 2: Courtney Brinegar
- Architect at Burdick Consultants, representing the **City of Conroe**.
- Announced an **open town hall meeting** for a feasibility study on Conroe's first official community center:
  - **Thursday, May 17, 4:30–6:30 PM** at the Oscar Johnson Community Center (100 Park Place).
  - An online survey is available on the City of Conroe website in English and Spanish.
- Noted youth-based programs will be included; encouraged CISD input.

### Speaker 3: Kristi Swoboda (TST/Conroe)
- Thanked the board for support of the **Salute to Education** event held the previous week.
- District Teacher of the Year winners: **Amy Kazi** (Conroe High School 9th) and **Hannah Janiszewski** (Runyon Elementary).
- Presented a **$1,500 check** to the **Conroe ISD Education Foundation** (correcting an earlier non-negotiable $500 check presented to Dr. Stockton at the event).

### Speaker 4: Ann Alexander Stone
- Longtime Conroe resident (40+ years); son is a CISD product; two granddaughters attend Runyon Elementary.
- Addressed a **social media rumor about Booker T. Washington Junior High School closing**.
- Spoke about the **legacy and community pride** associated with Booker T. Washington, naming numerous beloved educators.
- Expressed excitement about the **new school opening in 2020** and thanked Deputy Superintendent Curtis Snow for information about plans.
- Requested the board **"handle with care"** the building, name, and legacy of Booker T. Washington.
- Offered herself as a **community liaison** to help provide accurate information and counter rumors.

### Speaker 5: Teresa Wagaman
- PTO Programs Chair at **The Woodlands High School**.
- Praised the **CISD Police Department** for a school safety informational program held May 1st for PTO parents, organized after concerns following the Parkland, Florida shooting.
- Recognized **Chief Harness** (41 years of police service), **Sergeant Blakelock**, and **Sergeant Thompson** for their presentations on officer training, mental health identification, and cyber security.
- Encouraged all parents to **monitor children's cell phones and apps**.
- Noted the CISD Police Department is eager to present at any interested school.

---

## Item 5A: Naming — Principal of Hawk Academic Alternative High School

**Presenter:** Dr. Null (Deputy Superintendent)

**Recommendation:** **Dr. John Williams** as Principal of Hawk Academic Alternative High School

- Dr. Null described Hawk as a campus that "changes lives" and "changes generations."
- Praised Dr. Williams as having "a great heart for children" based on years of working alongside him.
- Dr. Williams thanked the board, recognized his wife and two daughters (both Conroe High School graduates), and pledged to "give students purpose, faith in their abilities, and a desire to succeed."

**Vote:** Approved (unanimous, voice vote — all in favor)

---

## Item 5B: Naming — Assistant Superintendent of Secondary Education

**Presenter:** Dr. Null

**Recommendation:** **Mr. Greg Colson** as Assistant Superintendent of Secondary Education

- Mr. Colson served 16 years as principal of **The Woodlands High School**.
- Dr. Null praised his visibility, love of children and staff, and noted he was the person who previously followed Dr. Stockton at The Woodlands.
- Mr. Colson thanked Dr. Stockton for 16 years of faith in his leadership, recognized his family (wife Sandra, sons David and Michael, their wives, and four grandchildren, with a fifth expected in September).
- Thanked Dr. Null for the opportunity.

**Vote:** Approved (unanimous, voice vote — all in favor)

---

## Item 6: Consent Agenda

- **Items 6E and 6F were removed** from the consent agenda at a board member's request for separate consideration.
- **Remaining consent agenda items approved.**

**Vote:** Approved (unanimous, voice vote — all in favor)

---

## Item 6E: Stanbury Uniforms Inc. — Marching Band and Concert Uniforms

- **Estimated expenditure:** $450,000
- Uniforms are part of the **lifecycle replacement schedule** and include uniforms for Grand Oaks High School.
- Programs receiving uniforms: Conroe High School Band & Orchestra, Oak Ridge High School Choir & Orchestra, Keeney Creek Orchestra, The Woodlands High School Orchestra.
- Funded from the general fund.
- Board member noted this was pulled for **transparency due to the large dollar figure** and to publicly express support for CISD's **fine arts programs**, described as among the most outstanding in the state.

**Vote:** Approved (unanimous, voice vote — all in favor)

---

## Item 6F: Paxton Patterson LLC — Junior High School Technology and Human Services Labs

- **Estimated annual expenditure:** $490,000
- Represents a **change in vendor** from the previous supplier.
- Paxton Patterson provides learning systems, tools, and instructional supplies aligned with **TEKS objectives**.
- Products allow for small group and individualized instruction.
- Labs will be implemented at **all seven junior high schools**.
- Staff confirmed the previous vendor's product was not meeting district needs and was shipping products the district was not satisfied with. A year-long evaluation confirmed Paxton Patterson as the better value.

**Vote:** Approved (unanimous, voice vote — all in favor)

---

## Item 7A: Capital Improvement Update

**Presenter:** Mr. Foster

### Projects on schedule:

| Project | Status/Details |
|---|---|
| **Grand Oaks High School** | Opening August 2018; landscape and interior finishing underway; CISD takes possession in July |
| **Katherine Johnson Clark Intermediate School** | Opening August 2018; interior finishing underway; possession in July |
| **Natatorium (Life Cycle)** | HVAC replacement; new ductwork installed; startup expected beginning of June |
| **Glenn Loch Elementary** | Reroofing underway; benefiting from good weather |
| **Life Cycle projects overall** | Will touch **31 campuses** this summer |
| **Woodforest Stadium scoreboard** | Structure complete; weld/windstorm inspections underway; scoreboard installation begins next week |
| **Moorhead Stadium scoreboard** | Foundation and structure installation underway; approximately 2 weeks behind Woodforest; both to be completed end of June |
| **Safety & Security project** | Contracts executed this week; camera/equipment orders being placed; most work above ceiling; on schedule |
| **Flex 19 (new campus)** | Opening August 2019; building pad complete; foundation construction starting in coming days |
| **Austin Elementary renovation** | Phase 1 underway; new detention/drainage and parking lots being built; project through all of 2019 |
| **Irons Junior High classroom addition** | Foundation/slab poured; structural steel arriving Monday; turnover planned for December 2018 winter break |
| **New Junior High (behind Bozeman)** | Site mobilized; clearing underway; opening August 2020 |
| **Conroe High School building addition** | Closing up exterior; near dry condition; turnover planned for December 2018 winter break; main building renovation to follow |

## Item 8A: Financial Reports

**Presenter:** Dr. Darren Rice

### General Fund:
- **Cash and investments:** $297 million total
  - Cash on hand: $500
  - Bank deposits: $959,000
  - State pool investments: $155 million
  - Short-term investments (<1 year): $89 million
  - Longer-term investments (TCG): $51.6 million
- Property tax collections slightly behind prior year but expected to reach 100%.
- **Projected fund balance increase** of ~$1.6 million to **$135.9 million** at 8/31/2018.

### Child Nutrition:
- Expected fund balance increase of ~$800,000.

### 2015 Bond Referendum:
- **Expended/encumbered:** $460 million
- **Estimate to complete:** ~$65.5 million
- **Total project forecast:** $525.5 million
- **Contingency remaining:** ~$2.9 million

### Self-Insurance Fund (April):
- Roughly break-even month.
- Year-to-date: revenues of $32 million vs. expenses of $28.3 million = **$3.7 million surplus**.
- Wellness center participation remains strong.
- **Note:** May expected to show higher claims due to preemie babies and other high-cost claims.

### Investment Portfolio:
- Par value: **$612 million**
- Pool yield: 1.94%
- Short-term yield: 1.81%
- Long-term yield: 1.46%
- Combined WAM: 88 days; yield: **1.88%** (vs. 90-day T-bill benchmark of 1.78%)

---

## Item 9A: Special District Recognition — Service to Conroe ISD (Dr. Don Stockton)

President Bush invited former and current board members to recognize **Dr. Don Stockton**, who is retiring after **15 years as superintendent** and **34+ years in public education**.

### Former Board Member Tributes:

**Dr. Ann Schneider:**
- Praised Dr. Stockton's integrity, moral character, and respect for everyone.
- Said he transformed the district so that "all schools are important" with strong leadership and financial foundation.
- Noted his impact on student confidence: "Students graduate with not only competence but with confidence."
- Thanked Kara Stockton for sharing her husband with the community.

**Mr. Mel Brown:**
- Described the crisis conditions when he joined the board (no superintendent, no fiscal officer, two lawsuits, questioned bond money).
- Was initially opposed to hiring Stockton as permanent superintendent but changed his mind after watching his leadership as interim.
- Called hiring Dr. Stockton "the best decision ever made as a school board member."

**Mr. Gerald Irons:**
- Praised Dr. Stockton's integrity, quality, and "Barnabas character" — the ability to see potential in people.
- Valued his honesty and willingness to give candid opinions.
- Commended how Stockton revitalized the curriculum.

**Mrs. Barbara Watson:**
- Quoted: "Power comes from values, from beliefs, from purpose, from within."
- Called out his integrity and dedication to "all means all."
- Thanked Kara Stockton.

**Mr. Bruce Tough:**
- Recalled the crisis period and inappropriate behavior by the prior superintendent.
- Called Stockton's integrity "impeccable" and "unimpeachable."
- Praised the district's combination of academic excellence, low costs, and transparency as a "triple crown."
- Credited Stockton with creating a "magnet for extraordinary people" to join the district.

**Mr. Joe Michaels:**
- Called Stockton "a man of the utmost charity and dignity."
- Thanked him on behalf of his three children in the district.

**Mr. CJ Haynes:**
- Recalled the tumultuous early board meetings (midnight adjournments, 75 speakers, divided votes).
- Called hiring Stockton "the proudest moment of my public career."
- Credited Stockton with teaching him patience and understanding.

**Mrs. Linda Sasser:**
- Praised Stockton's vision, communication, community alignment, and 24/7 commitment.
- Highlighted programs like Reading for a Better Life and Field Trip Friday.
- Said "it was all about him — his leadership and 24/7 commitment mattered."

**Dr. Alan Moore:**
- Noted the district was already good before Stockton but "nobody knew it."
- Credited Stockton with telling the story and expanding on what was already excellent.
- Shared personal family connection to Don and Kara Stockton going back decades to their parents.

### Current Board Member Tributes:

**Dr. Skeeter Hubert:**
- Shared a humorous personal story about first meeting Dr. Stockton at a "Men Who Cook" event.
- Praised his consistent humility and willingness to give time to anyone.

**Mr. Ray Sanders:**
- Read an extended tribute highlighting Stockton's respect, understanding, acceptance, and appreciation for all (quoting Mahatma Gandhi).
- Referenced Philippians 2:3 regarding valuing others above yourself.
- Praised the Reading for a Better Life program, annual convocation words, and Stockton's role in making CISD "the example for other schools to follow."
- Offered personal friendship and golf tee times in retirement.
- Thanked Kara Stockton.

**Mr. Dale Inman:**
- Shared a story of witnessing Dr. Stockton's personal impact on a former student who was bullied — Stockton had walked her to class daily, something the now-adult parent remembered years later.
- Called Stockton a mentor and friend who made him "a better boss, a better leader, and a better person."

**Mr. Scott Kidd:**
- Compared Dr. Stockton to **George Bailey** from *It's a Wonderful Life* (Stockton's stated role model).
- Drew parallels to decisiveness, empathy, dedication, servant leadership, and faith.
- Thanked Kara Stockton.

**Mr. John Husbands:**
- Reflected on knowing Stockton since their children were in nursery together.
- Said "Don didn't become special when he became superintendent — Don was special before."
- Praised transparency, leadership in principal selection, and making zoning painless.
- Thanked Stockton for "brilliant leadership and exemplary service."

**Mr. Datren Williams:**
- Shortest-tenured board member but longtime community volunteer.
- Praised the culture of accessibility — Stockton, Dr. Hines, Dr. Null, and Darren Rice never said "no" to a meeting request.
- Noted that when he announced his candidacy, his motivation was "I want to be a part of that."
- Said the entire community is better for the strength Stockton gave the district.
- Quoted the "three pillars" of a strong community: business, faith, and schools.

**President Melanie Bush:**
- Said Stockton's legacy is in every teacher who returned to CISD, every classroom where a child feels cared for, every hallway smile and kind word.
- "You sir have made Conroe ISD a wonderful place to work and a wonderful environment for all our kids to learn."

---

## Item 9B: Dr. Stockton's Farewell Remarks

- Recognized his wife **Kara Stockton**.
- Reflected on his start as interim superintendent in 2003 and sending the first-ever email from a superintendent to district staff.
- Noted he has presided over **hundreds of board meetings** and shaken the hands of over **50,000 graduates**.
- Thanked former and current board members for always putting children first, valuing employees, being good community members, and being good stewards of taxpayer money.
- Thanked them for challenging him and holding him accountable.
- Individually recognized:
  - **Cathy** (longtime staff member receiving recognition)
  - **Kari** (leadership and "spirited arguments")
  - **Chris Hines** ("like the brother I wish I had")
  - **Dr. Stuart** (community planning/zoning work)
  - **Chief Harness** (41 years of police service)
- Introduced all former board members present: Dr. Mel Brown, CJ Haynes, Chris Irish, Gerald Irons, Dr. Kauffman (Jovi), Joe Michaels, Dr. Alan Moore, Linda Sasser, Dr. Ann Schneider, Bruce Tough, Barbara Watson.
- Introduced current board members: Melanie Bush, Ray Sanders, Dr. Skeeter Hubert, Datren Williams, Scott Kidd, John Husbands, Scott Moore.
- Closing quote: *"I've been asked many times if my career was a dream come true and the answer's no — not even a dream could have turned out like this."*

---

## Adjournment

**Motion to adjourn:** Made and seconded.
**Meeting adjourned.**
